name: Niagara Regional
description_short: Niagara Regional Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving 2,700+ members with $34M+ in assets, offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards since 1939.
description_long: Niagara Regional Federal Credit Union was chartered in May 1939 by a group of teachers seeking a not-for-profit alternative to traditional banking. Over 80+ years, it has grown from a paper-based operation to a fully computerized institution with multiple locations in Amherst and North Tonawanda, NY, while maintaining its core mission of personalized member service. The credit union serves over 2,700 members with assets exceeding $34 million. The institution offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, home equity lines of credit, new Visa credit cards, student loans, and online/mobile banking services. Members can access online banking, mobile apps, bill pay, money transfers, and loan payment services. Current advertised rates include auto loans at 3.99% APR, personal loans at 10.00% APR, and Visa credit cards at 13.99% APR. As a credit union, Niagara Regional differentiates itself as a not-for-profit member-owned institution rather than a profit-driven bank. This structure allows them to return earnings to members through lower loan rates, higher savings yields, and personalized service. The organization emphasizes individualized banking experiences adapted to members' life stages and financial goals, with staff education focused on personal-level guidance. Niagara Regional is best suited for individuals seeking community-based banking with competitive rates and personal service rather than national chains. Their member-owned structure and $34M asset base position them as a stable regional alternative, though their geographic limitation to upstate New York may restrict accessibility for non-local members despite digital banking options.
pros: Member-owned not-for-profit structure returns earnings through lower loan rates and higher savings yields
Over 80 years of operational history demonstrates institutional stability and longevity
Competitive rate offerings: 3.99% auto loans, 10.00% personal loans, 13.99% Visa credit cards
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, home equity, student loans, and credit cards
Digital-first services with online banking, mobile app, and remote account management
Personal service model with staff education focused on individual member needs
Clear fraud security protocols with direct member communication about scam threats
cons: Geographic limitation to Amherst and North Tonawanda, NY restricts in-person access for remote members
Smaller institution ($34M assets, 2,700 members) offers fewer branch locations than larger regional/national competitors
Limited information about membership eligibility criteria and field of membership on website
No mention of specific account fees, minimum balance requirements, or fee schedules in provided content
Mobile app and online banking capabilities described broadly without detailed feature lists
best_for: Teachers and education workers (original founding member base, ongoing service focus)
Upstate New York residents in Niagara County seeking personalized, community-based banking
Borrowers prioritizing competitive loan rates over national bank convenience
Members valuing not-for-profit ownership structure and member-centric earnings reinvestment
diagnosis: Niagara Regional is best for upstate New York residents seeking stable, member-owned banking with competitive rates and personalized service. The primary caveat is geographic accessibility—membership and in-person services are limited to the Amherst and North Tonawanda area, making it less practical for non-local consumers despite available digital banking options.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Niagara Regional Federal Credit Union serves 2,700+ members with checking, savings, loans, and credit cards.
